mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW X3 3.0d / 3.0sd engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned X3 3.0d/3.0sd engine typically costs £2,000–£5,500 supply only depending on generation. Fitting adds £1,500–£2,500 depending on location and workshop rates. A used N57D30O1 engine costs from £2,495 supply only with fitting around £1,150. A complete N57 swap typically costs around £5,000–£6,000 all-in. Main dealer new engines start from £9,000–£13,000+ supply only. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW X3 3.0d / 3.0sd engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends entirely on which engine generation your X3 has. The M57 (E83) is widely regarded as one of BMW's most durable diesels — timing chain replacement is recommended between 250,000–300,000 km. The N57 (F25) brought refinement but has more documented timing chain and turbo issues. The B57 (G01) introduced improved timing chain durability. Check the Era Map above for your specific car.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW X3 3.0d / 3.0sd?

acceptedAnswer.text: Turbocharger failures — common on both 2.0- and 3.0-litre units due to blocked oil feed pipes, timing chain wear (especially on N57 engines), injector and high-pressure fuel pump issues (N57 and B57), and DPF clogging from short urban journeys.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW X3 3.0d / 3.0sd engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and condition. Against a well-kept £5,000–£15,000 X3, a £3,500–£5,500 recon is 25–110% of the car's value. For a clean E83 M57 example, replacement is almost always worth it given the engine's legendary durability. For F25 N57 examples with no chain history, evaluate the car's overall condition before committing.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW X3 3.0d / 3.0sd engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. As a rule of thumb: E83 (2004–2010) has M57D30, F25 (2011–2017) has N57D30A/O1, G01 (2017–present) has B57D30O0/B. Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the N57 timing chain, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. A preventative chain replacement (£1,200–£3,000) is far cheaper than a catastrophic chain snap (£4,500–£8,000 engine replacement). At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately. The M57 timing chain is recommended to be replaced between 250,000–300,000 km, while the B57 has improved chain durability with a 200,000 km interval.

eraMap.title: BMW X3 3.0d / 3.0sd — Which Engine Does Your Car Actually Have?

rows.eraNote: 3.0L single-turbo straight-six — 204–218 hp. Very robust, timing chain can last 250,000–300,000 km
3.0L twin-turbo — 286 hp, 580 Nm. Rare high-output variant. Similar durability to 3.0d
3.0L single/twin-turbo — 258–313 hp. More refined than M57, timing chain less robust
3.0L — 265–326 hp, MHEV on later models. Modern B57 with improved timing chain durability

replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an X3 3.0d/3.0sd engine typically takes 12–16 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer). Timing chain replacement on rear-chain engines (E83 M57, F25 N57) is particularly labour-intensive.

cta.label: → Compare X3 3.0d / 3.0sd engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: Turbocharger Failure (All Generations)
problems.symptoms: Loss of power, excessive smoke from exhaust, unusual whistling or siren-like noises from the engine bay, engine warning light

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if you hear whistling or experience power loss

problems.recommendation: Turbocharger failures are common on both 2.0- and 3.0-litre diesel X3s, mainly as a result of oil feed supply pipes becoming blocked over time. The N57 engine is particularly prone to turbocharger failures, with symptoms including loss of power, excessive smoke, and unusual noises. If you experience these symptoms, have the turbo system inspected immediately — a £800–£2,500 repair is far cheaper than the engine damage that can follow if ignored. For M57 engines, wastegate rattle is a known issue.

failureLink.label: Read the full turbo failure guide

problems.title: Timing Chain Wear (M57, N57 & Early B57)
problems.symptoms: Rattling noise on cold start, metallic ticking from the rear of the engine (M57/N57) or front (B57), engine warning light, loss of power

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattle on cold start, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: While the M57 is robust, the timing chain on these engines is recommended to be replaced between 250,000–300,000 km. On the N57, timing chain issues are more common. The B57 has improved chain durability but the recommended replacement interval is still 200,000 km. At the first sign of cold-start rattling, get the chain inspected immediately — a £1,200–£3,000 preventative repair is dramatically cheaper than a £4,500–£8,000 engine replacement.

failureLink.label: Read the full timing chain failure guide

problems.title: Injector & High-Pressure Fuel Pump (HPFP) Issues
problems.symptoms: Rough idling, poor fuel economy, increased emissions, misfires, hard starting, loss of power
problems.recommendation: For N57 and B57 engines, failing high-pressure fuel pumps (OEM 13517603799) and carbon-clogged injectors (OEM 13537598421) are common issues. The N57 engine is also prone to injector problems. Fuel injector failures can lead to rough idling, poor fuel economy, and increased emissions. If you experience rough running or increased fuel consumption, have the injectors and HPFP tested. Catching these issues early prevents more serious engine damage.

failureLink.label: Read the full injector failure guide

buyingChecks.title: Buying This X3 3.0d / 3.0sd? Check These First
buyIf: * E83 M57-engined car (pre-2010) with the robust six-cylinder — these are the most durable
* B57-engined car (2017+) with improved timing chain durability
* Documented timing chain history on any N57-engined car (F25, 2011–2017)
* Full service history with BMW Longlife-04 oil specification
avoidIf: * N57-engined car with cold-start rattle and no chain history — this is a ticking time bomb
* Any engine with no turbo service history — turbo failures are common
* Poor service history — diesel BMWs are unforgiving of neglect
* DPF warning lights with no diagnosis

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The X3 3.0d and 3.0sd badges span three very different engine generations. The M57 (E83) is widely regarded as one of BMW's most durable diesels — timing chain replacement is recommended between 250,000–300,000 km. The N57 (F25) brought refinement but has more documented timing chain and turbo issues. The B57 (G01) introduced improved timing chain durability. Buy an E83 M57 if you want the most durable option, or a G01 B57 if you want modern efficiency with better chain reliability. Buy on documented history, not on generation alone.

cta.label: → Compare X3 3.0d / 3.0sd engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW X3 3.0d or 3.0sd (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling or ticking (timing chain warning — this is critical)
* Turbo Health — Listen for whistling or siren-like noises under acceleration; check for power loss
* Service History — Including which engine era (M57, N57 or B57) and confirmed oil-interval discipline
* Timing Chain History — Has the chain been replaced? M57: 250,000–300,000 km interval
* Injector / HPFP Health — Any rough idle, misfires, or increased fuel consumption?
* DPF Health — Check for DPF warning lights; short journeys are a red flag
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Look for turbo, timing or fuel system codes
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
